LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT KENNETH WALCZAK'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS FOLLOWING SUCCESSFUL ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, WITHOUT ANY BREAKDOWN OF ANY KIND, CONCERNING HOW OR ON WHAT BASIS THE TIME WAS SPENT, THE COURT CANNOT EVALUATE OR ARRIVED A REASONABLE FEE AWARD, ESPECIALLY GIVEN THE RELATIVELY HIGH AMOUNT OF AWARD SOUGHT, AND THE SEQUENTIAL INVOLVEMENT OF 2 FIRMS. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWING ON AN ADEQUATE FACTUAL RECORD. THE COURT ALSO CONFIRMED THAT DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO FEES. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: JOYCE D. LONG, CSR # 8620

ATTY. ALEX A. GRAFT OF LEWIS, BRISBOIS & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6692 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KENNETH WALCZAK'S Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs Following Succesful Anti-Slapp Motion, WITHOUT ANY BREAKDOWN OF ANY KIND, CONCERNING HOW OR ON WHAT BASIS THE TIME WAS SPENT, THE COURT CANNOT EVALUATE OR ARRIVED A REASONABLE FEE AWARD, ESPECIALLY GIVEN THE RELATIVELY HIGH AMOUNT OF AWARD SOUGHT, AND THE SEQUENTIAL INVOLVEMENT OF 2 FIRMS. THEREFORE, THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWING ON AN ADEQUATE FACTUAL RECORD. THE COURT ALSO CONFIRMED THAT DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO FEES. Off Calendar Feb-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHARON L. CESAR, TEL. NO. (510)528-1640 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
